lastReleaseMessage: Course-refresh cron moved off GitHub Actions onto local Task Scheduler.

barRaise.topFinding: The working tree has become a holding pen and it is now actively dangerous. Two full version cuts sit authored-but-uncommitted (the 0.33.1 cron patch and a new 0.34.0 MINOR that touches real worker code in worker/src/index.js plus src/profile.ts and a new force_refresh_courses.py), while HEAD is still v0.33.0 from 2026-05-25, so roughly four days of work sit on the FUSE mount in the exact silent-truncation window Test-YacIntegrity exists to catch. Worse, the action that flushes that risk also breaks the dashboard: scripts/write-dashboard-status.ps1 rebuilds the status JSON from scratch and hardcodes audit.findings to 0/0/0 (line 98) while nulling the entire barRaise block (lines 103-110), so running release.ps1 will silently erase this bar-raise and zero the audit counts on the dashboard. Fix the dashboard writer to merge, then ship the release. Separately, the Decision 26 slot collision relocated rather than closing: DECISIONS_NEEDED.md now holds two competing Decision 27 headings.
